description: Lists properties and responses of pile elements in the range. If no keyword is given, the default output of the command is that associated with the information keyword.

keywords.name: apply
keywords.description: uniform applied distributed load acting on the element

keywords.name: coupling-confinement
keywords.description: confining stress acting perpendicular to the element, values acting on the local y and z directions

keywords.name: coupling-yield
keywords.description: yield state of the coupling springs in the normal and shear directions, and the current gap values

keywords.name: force-node-global
keywords.description: generalized nodal forces (force and moment) acting on the element. These are the generalized forces exerted by the nodes on the element. express the values in the element coordinate system (the default) express the values in the global coordinate system

keywords.name: group
keywords.description: group assignment(s). If slot is specified, then list the group assignment, if any, in slot s .

keywords.name: information
keywords.description: general element information: nodal connectivity, centroid, surface area, volume, and hide/select status

keywords.name: plastic-state
keywords.description: Plasticity state indicators. Each beam element with a plastic material model has a number of integration points distributed throughout its volume, and each of them tracks its plasticity state. The plasticity states at all integration points are listed, unless integration-point is specified. The integration point locations are shown here and here . The plasticity states of the plastic constitutive models can be listed by the structure pile cmodel list states command.

keywords.name: plastic-stress
keywords.description: Stress for elements with a plastic material model. The plasticity is induced by axial and bending deformations; twisting deformation induces an elastic response. Each beam element with a plastic material model has a number of integration points distributed throughout its volume, and each of them tracks its axial stress. The axial stress is the only non-zero stress component arising from axial and bending deformation. The axial stress ( \(\sigma_{xx}\) ) is expressed in the beam element coordinate system. The axial stress at all integration points is listed, unless integration-point is specified. The integration point locations are shown here and here .

keywords.name: plastic-stress-bounds
keywords.description: minimum and maximum axial stress for elements with a plastic material model by checking over all integration points.

keywords.name: property-yield-tension
keywords.description: The properties consist of constitutive model properties, beam-type properties, coupling-spring properties and rockbolt properties. The constitutive model properties are listed by specifying the constitutive property name; these names are not listed here. Elastic-plastic constitutive model properties are stored at each integration point. Such properties are the same at all integration points, except for the softening properties of the strain-softening/hardening Mohr Coulomb constitutive model. The value at a particular integration point is listed by specifying the integration-point keyword — the integration point locations are shown here and here . Scaled properties are returned if the scaled keyword ( FLAC2D ONLY ) is set to true (the default is false). If the property cannot be scaled, an error is emitted. Beam-Type Properties cross-sectional area mass density direction-y vector whose projection onto the element cross-section defines the \(y\) -axis of the element coordinate system moment of inertia with respect to element \(y\) -axis moment of inertia with respect to element \(z\) -axis plastic moment capacity in Y and Z plastic moment capacity in Y plastic moment capacity in Z plastic shear capacity in Y and Z plastic shear capacity in Y plastic shear capacity in Z shear coefficient for transverse shear deformation in the \(y\) -direction (Timoshenko beam only) shear coefficient for transverse shear deformation in the \(z\) -direction (Timoshenko beam only) thermal-expansion coefficient torsion constant Coupling-Spring Properties normal coupling spring cohesion per unit length shear coupling spring cohesion per unit length normal coupling spring friction angle shear coupling spring friction angle normal coupling spring gap-use flag normal coupling spring stiffness shear coupling spring stiffness exposed perimeter large-strain sliding flag large-strain sliding tolerance Rockbolt Properties name of the table relating cohesion of shear coupling spring to relative shear displacement flag to activate incremental confining stress logic name of the table relating effective confining stress factor to deviatoric stress name of the table relating friction angle of shear coupling spring to relative shear displacement flag to activate rockbolt logic tensile failure strain (non-dimensional, only active if rockbolt-flag is on ) axial compressive yield strength (force unit, only active if rockbolt-flag is on ) axial tensile yield strength (force unit, only active if rockbolt-flag is on )
